summary: Null assertions and unclear naming create fragile and hard-to-maintain logic.

findings.evidence: x uses u!! and email!!, allowing runtime failures instead of explicit null boundary handling.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace non-null assertions with explicit null-safe branches or require non-null input at API boundary.
findings.verification: Add tests for null user/null email paths and verify deterministic error or fallback behavior.

findings.evidence: Method/parameter/local names x, u, and e hide intent and make null-boundary reasoning harder.
findings.minimal_fix: Rename to domain terms (e.g., extractDomain(user), user, email) and keep boundary checks explicit.
findings.verification: Run tests and code review checklist to confirm names communicate behavior and null expectations.
